codeReview.summary: The implementation is minimal, correct, and exactly matches the feature spec. A single `<link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="/favicon.png" />` line was added to `frontend/index.html` inside the `<head>` block, immediately after the `<title>` tag. The pre-existing `frontend/public/favicon.png` binary (9,896 bytes) was committed alongside. The link tag attributes match exactly what the feature description prescribes. All existing tests passed (272 backend, 80 frontend), svelte-check reported 0 errors, and the production build succeeded. Browser verification via agent-browser confirmed the favicon served with HTTP 200 and no console errors were present.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill's Step 7 says to start the dev server with `cd frontend && npm run dev` on port 5173, but the project's `.factory/services.yaml` declares `vite-dev` on port 5199 (`npx vite --port 5199`). The worker correctly used port 5199 (from services.yaml and mission AGENTS.md), but the skill's hardcoded port 5173 causes a mismatch. The skill should reference `.factory/services.yaml` for the port, or at minimum say 'use the port declared in services.yaml' rather than hardcoding 5173.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 7: 'Start the dev server: `cd frontend && npm run dev` (port 5173)'. services.yaml vite-dev.port: 5199. Worker transcript: worker attempted --strictPort on 5199, found it occupied, and reused the existing session's dev server. skillFeedback.deviations confirms the mismatch.

summary: Clean pass. The favicon link tag was added correctly to index.html with the exact attributes specified in the feature description. The favicon.png asset was committed, tests and type checks passed, and browser verification confirmed the favicon loads with no errors. The only observation is a minor skill maintenance gap: the frontend-worker skill hardcodes port 5173 for the dev server, while the project's services.yaml declares port 5199.
